items.title: APAC FX: Balance-of-payments strains shape flows – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Geoff Yu highlights that APAC balance-of-payments pressures from the Iran conflict and energy by-products are increasingly driving currency flows, with MYR, THB, AUD and PHP in focus.

items.title: Indonesia: Cautious reopening under geopolitical strain – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Radhika Rao discusses Indonesia’s onshore markets as they reopen after the Lebaran holiday to a backdrop of uncertain geopolitics and volatile global sentiment.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar holds firm below 100 as Iran tensions keep markets on edge
items.summary: Markets were driven by a shift in geopolitical sentiment as reports of potential ceasefire talks initially eased tensions. However, uncertainty quickly resurfaced after Iran signaled reluctance to engage with the United States, highlighting fragile diplomatic progress and keeping markets on edge.

items.title: NZD/USD declines for second day as Middle East tensions lift US Dollar
items.summary: NZD/USD trades lower on Wednesday around 0.5820, down 0.22% on the day, extending its decline for a second straight day. The downside move is mainly driven by the strength of the US Dollar (USD), supported by a broader risk-off environment.

items.title: Euro area: Resilient NPL ratios face 2026 energy shock – BNP Paribas
items.summary: BNP Paribas argues that European Union (EU) manufacturing firms enter the 2026 energy shock from Iran with historically low non-performing loan (NPL) ratios, suggesting stronger financial health than in 2022.

items.title: Australia: Energy costs keep CPI elevated – UOB
items.summary: UOB economist Lee Sue Ann highlights that Australian inflation remains elevated, with housing and electricity key drivers, even as trimmed mean CPI tracks slightly below earlier RBA projections.

items.title: Denmark: Government formation faces prolonged negotiations – Nordea
items.summary: Nordea’s Group Chief Economist Helge J. Pedersen notes that the Danish parliamentary election has produced a highly fragmented Folketing, with 12 parties entering parliament and no majority for either the red or blue bloc. The Moderates, led by Lars Løkke Rasmussen, now hold the balance of power.

items.title: Oil stabilizes after losses as US-Iran talks ease supply fears, inventories rise
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) trades around $88.20 on Wednesday at the time of writing, up 0.40% on the day, attempting to stabilize after a recent pullback.

items.title: Fed: Oil shock complicates rate path – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that the Federal Reserve (Fed) faces conflicting signals as the Iran conflict drives an Oil shock.

items.title: USD: Upside risk persists in conflict-driven markets – BBH
items.summary: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes that global risk sentiment has improved as markets position for a potential conflict resolution involving Iran, with DXY consolidating below 100.00.

items.title: GBP: BoE hawkish shift supports Pound – UOB
items.summary: UOB economist Lee Sue Ann highlights a hawkish pivot by the Bank of England (BoE), with the Bank Rate held at 3.75% and a unanimous 9–0 vote. The report removes prior expectations for three 2026 cuts, now forecasting the GBP Repo Rate steady at 3.75% through 4Q26 as inflation risks dominate.

items.title: Oil: Supply shock deepens as Hormuz stays constrained – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities’ Senior Commodity Strategist Ryan McKay warns that Oil markets face escalating tightness as flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain severely restricted and Gulf production cuts exceed 10m b/d.
